description: Designing full GNC architecture for RPO satellite, integrating CV-based relative pose sensors with optimal guidance and navigation algorithms for autonomous rendezvous. Built a real-time HPOP propagator (20 cm error per orbit) and developed EKF/UKF for state estimation, achieving 5 cm GNSS-only accuracy.

Researched novel autonomous, robust, and highly accurate GNC solutions for robotic lunar landers. Successfully engineered the first efficient, mathematically-robust optimal control algorithm for lunar landing.
Spearheaded research on VLEO (Very Low Earth Orbit) concepts, catalyzing a full-team project. Designed an optimization algorithm to parametrize subsystems, achieving an 80% satellite mass reduction.

role: Strategy Officer & Co-founder. Levitation, Dynamics and Braking Lead
description: Co-founded and led a team of 120+ students to develop a Hyperloop prototype. Optimized key components (Hallback array, pod suspension) using MATLAB and SIMULINK to enhance system performance.
